Optimal Allocation to Cryptocurrencies in Diversified Portfolios

Shannon's Demon on Shitters: Volatility Harvesting on Crypto Perps
short_description: Margin Syndicate describes a constant-mix / Shannon's-demon rebalancing structure run at derivatives leverage on netted margin across crypto alts, where a constant-gross long+short position rebalances to a target ratio at every price threshold so volatility itself becomes the revenue line. It runs in three regime-driven configurations: a neutral 50/50 book that harvests chop, a signal book with a directional tilt (e.g. 60/40) on confirmation, and a depressed-asset book with a modest long tilt on beaten-down names where negative funding pays you to wait.

title: Systematic BTC Options Report: Performance Model and Forward Projections
short_description: A six-page Margin Syndicate report presenting a performance model and forward Monte Carlo projection for a systematic short-volatility BTC options strategy on Deribit (short strangles, iron condors, spreads), delta-hedged via perps on 5-minute cycles. Modeled from live production parameters, it reports a Sharpe of 5.83, max drawdown of -1.33%, and a six-month median projected return of +13% from a 10,000-path fat-tail (Student-t) simulation, while stressing the figures are modeled/projected rather than audited live returns.

title: Systematic Market-Neutral Crypto: A Readable Statistical Signal
short_description: Talha Çağrı Kotcioğlu shares a live-deployed market-neutral crypto strategy (Sharpe 3.45, Sortino 4.42, profit factor 1.80, max drawdown 7.2%) built and validated on two years of tick-level data with all costs baked in, no ML and no leverage, tolerant of up to one hour of execution delay. Per the author's comments, its edge is a crash-convex 'pre-sizing and parity choosing' mechanism that tilts heavily onto shorts ahead of stress so liquidation cascades drive PnL while flat markets bleed ~1-2%; the discussion also collects strong skeptic pushback that a two-year backtest with six-month out-of-sample is under one market cycle and prone to regime breakdown.

title: The Low-Volatility Factor in Cryptocurrency Asset Selection
Update on the Majors: Moving Grid Capital Off BTC and ETH
short_description: Steven Paterson (Margin Syndicate) explains a structural reallocation of grid-trading capital off BTC and ETH because the majors now trade identically to ES and Nasdaq futures — a managed short-vol regime that pays theta but destroys grids, which need independent two-sided price action. The resulting split: options carry the majors while grids trade alt perpetuals, where independent operators, idiosyncratic funding and genuine two-sided volatility keep a mean-reversion edge alive.

title: A Simple Market Making Bot for 01 Exchange
An Efficient Algorithm for Optimal Routing Through Constant Function Market Makers
short_description: This paper presents a decomposition-based algorithm for optimally routing a trade across a network of constant function market makers (CFMMs) such as Uniswap to maximize a user-specified utility function. The method naturally handles complex and aggregate CFMMs and demonstrates significant performance improvements over commercial solvers on realistic DEX networks.

title: An LVR Approach Proof of Guillaume Lambert's Uniswap V3 Implied Volatility
Arbitrage Opportunities and Efficiency Tests in Crypto Derivatives
Automated Market Making and Arbitrage Profits in the Presence of Fees
short_description: This paper extends a model of two-asset automated market makers to incorporate trading fees and discrete Poisson block generation times, deriving the expected instantaneous arbitrage profit rate in closed form. The key finding is that in the low-fee, fast-block limit, fees reduce arbitrage profits by a simple multiplicative factor equal to the fraction of time a profitable trade exists.

Bank Stocks and Multifactor Models
authors: James W. Kolari, Wei Liu, Huiling Liao, Hilal Anwar Butt
short_description: Kolari, Liu, Liao and Butt use Fama-MacBeth cross-sectional regressions on CRSP daily bank-stock returns (1986-2023) to test whether multifactor models built for nonfinancial stocks can price bank stocks, comparing the CAPM and five multifactor models against the two-factor Mean Variance Pricing (MVP) model (market beta risk plus market-return-dispersion 'zeta' risk). Adding size, value, profitability, investment and momentum factors substantially improves pricing, but the parsimonious two-factor MVP model outperforms on goodness-of-fit and factor-loading significance, reaching an out-of-sample R-squared near 99% on size-sorted bank portfolios with zeta t-statistics above 4 — implying bank stocks are less anomalous than previously believed.

The skewness-managed portfolios raise annual returns by an average of 5.45 percentage points, improve Sharpe ratios by 0.12, and retain significant alphas after standard factor-model and transaction-cost tests.

short_description: This paper develops a backtesting framework for concentrated liquidity market makers (CLMMs) on Uniswap V3 using a parametric model to approximate the liquidity distribution within pools. Testing on historical 2023 data across altcoin, stablecoin, and USDC/ETH pools with various fee levels, the framework achieves less than 1% error in modeling pool rewards, demonstrating its utility for LP revenue estimation and strategy evaluation.

title: Earning Yield on Bitcoin via Leveraged WBTC/cbBTC Liquidity Provision on Fluid
short_description: An X thread by letsgetonchain explaining a leveraged "looping" liquidity-provision strategy that earns ~16% trailing 30-day APY on Bitcoin by providing WBTC/cbBTC liquidity on the Fluid DEX using smart-collateral and smart-debt primitives. The yield comes from ~0.7% average trading-fee APY amplified by ~18x leverage; liquidation risk is minimal because both tokens are oracle-marked at 1 BTC, with the main risk being a spike in BTC borrow rates on Fluid.

title: Opportunity Costs and Profitability: Insights into LP Behavior
Providing Liquidity in Uniswap V3
short_description: This ETH Zurich bachelor's thesis builds a precise backtester based on the original Uniswap V3 smart contract and uses it to evaluate eleven concentrated liquidity provision strategies on the USDC-ETH pool with a 0.05% fee. The study finds that strategies perform well when ETH price is rising but poorly in down markets, and also analyzes the delta exposure of different liquidity provision approaches.

title: What Drives Liquidity on Decentralized Exchanges? Evidence from the Uniswap Protocol
short_description: This paper studies the determinants of liquidity on decentralized exchanges using Uniswap data, introducing the v2 counterfactual spread metric to decompose market depth into total value locked (TVL) and concentration channels. Key findings include that gas prices, returns, and trading volume share affect liquidity through concentration, private market maker internalization affects TVL but not overall depth, and volatility and fee revenue affect both channels.

title: A CNN-LSTM Stock Prediction Algorithm
A Deep Learning Based Asset Allocation Methodology For Investment Portfolio Optimization Under Uncertainties
short_description: This paper combines Modern Portfolio Theory with a deep learning model to determine optimal asset allocation within diversified investment portfolios, incorporating both historical correlations and current market dynamics. Using the S&P 500 as a benchmark, the methodology aims to achieve comparable returns while minimizing risk through strategic multi-asset combination, moving beyond purely historical data-driven approaches.

title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for the Financial Portfolio Management Problem
short_description: This paper presents a model-free reinforcement learning framework for cryptocurrency portfolio management using an Ensemble of Identical Independent Evaluators (EIIE) topology with CNN, RNN, and LSTM instantiations. In backtests with 30-minute trading periods across cryptocurrencies, all three instances achieve at least 4-fold returns in 50 days at a 0.25% commission rate, outperforming ten alternative portfolio strategies.

short_description: This paper proposes a Deep Q-Network (DQN) framework for multi-asset portfolio management that discretizes the action space as portfolio weights and combines CNN with dueling DQN to enhance signal recognition. Tested on five low-correlated US stocks, the DQN strategy produces 30% more profit than ten traditional strategies while achieving the lowest risk of policy as measured by Sharpe ratio relative to Max Drawdown.

The study applies various resampling strategies to address class imbalance in cryptocurrency return prediction, evaluating ensemble classifiers to improve the robustness and profitability of trading signals across multiple cryptocurrencies.

title: Data-Driven Duration Management — Term Structure Forecasting Using Machine Learning
authors: Tobias Lausser, Joao Eduardo Vuolo, Rudi Zagst
short_description: This paper benchmarks classical term-structure models (Dynamic Nelson-Siegel, PCA with AR/VAR factor dynamics) against neural network architectures for forecasting U.S. and European zero-coupon government bond yield curves, evaluating forecasts with both statistical accuracy (RMSE, MAE, directional accuracy) and an economic bond-trading strategy (Information Ratio, Omega ratio, Maximum Drawdown). Neural networks consistently outperform traditional models on both fronts; the best U.S. model is a direct-forecasting NN combining DNS factors with an autoencoder for macroeconomic features, while the best European model is a factor-based NN on PCA rate factors without macro inputs. All six selected NN models beat a five-year constant-duration benchmark with a positive Information Ratio, whereas classical models were filtered out for failing to produce actionable trading signals.

title: Deep Learning Applying on Stock Trading
short_description: This Stanford CS230 project applies two deep learning approaches to stock trading: an LSTM time-series forecasting model for price prediction and a reinforcement learning model for direct portfolio action selection. Using S&P 500 top-capitalization stocks from 2000-2020 with technical indicators as features, both methods are evaluated on 2019-2020 out-of-sample data to compare indirect and direct trading approaches.

title: Deep Learning for High-Frequency Price Prediction in Cryptocurrency Markets
Deep Reinforcement Learning for Financial Portfolio Optimisation
Deep Reinforcement Trading with Predictable Returns
short_description: This paper investigates model-free deep reinforcement learning (DRL) traders in a controlled market environment with known mean-reverting factors, allowing direct comparison of DRL performance against an exact dynamic programming solution. The study shows that DRL agents leveraging classical mean-reversion strategies outperform benchmarks even when price dynamics are misspecified or assumptions about extreme events and volatility clustering are violated.

title: Dependency structures in cryptocurrency market from high to low frequency
short_description: This paper investigates logarithmic return cross-correlations across 25 liquid cryptocurrencies on FTX at time horizons from 15 seconds to 1 day, using Minimum Spanning Trees and Triangulated Maximally Filtered Graphs to map hierarchical dependency structures. The study finds that cross-correlations decrease at finer time resolutions and that a growing hierarchical structure emerges at coarser resolutions, with mainstream cryptocurrencies playing evolving leadership roles.

Its memorable illustration: tick bars reveal a ~10% flash rally and flash crash hidden in the time-bar representation, motivating activity-based sampling. Archived as an original summary card.

short_description: A practical Towards Data Science tutorial on imbalance bars (tick/volume/dollar: TIB/VIB/DIB), the most information-driven member of the López de Prado bar family. It explains the mechanism — accumulate signed-tick imbalance and close a bar when it breaches an expected threshold (EWMA of bar size times EWMA of imbalance proportion), so aggressive one-sided flow samples faster — and is candid about implementation pitfalls. Archived as an original summary card rather than a reproduction of the source article.

short_description: This paper documents significant time series momentum in 58 liquid futures instruments across equity index, currency, commodity, and bond markets, finding that the past 12-month excess return positively predicts future returns over 1-12 month horizons before partially reversing. A diversified time series momentum portfolio delivers substantial abnormal returns with low exposure to standard asset pricing factors and performs best during extreme markets, with speculators profiting at the expense of hedgers.

title: On technical trading and social media indicators for cryptocurrency price classification through deep learning
short_description: This paper compares four deep learning algorithms (MLP, CNN, LSTM, ALSTM) for classifying Bitcoin and Ethereum price movements using technical, trading, and social media indicators over 2017-2020. The key finding is that incorporating trading and social media indicators alongside technical variables (the unrestricted model) significantly improves prediction accuracy consistently across all algorithms compared to technical indicators alone.

short_description: This bachelor's thesis from Blekinge Institute of Technology compares multiple machine learning algorithms for predicting cryptocurrency prices, evaluating their relative performance across different model architectures. The study conducts a comparative analysis of prediction accuracy and error metrics to identify which ML approaches are best suited for the highly volatile cryptocurrency market.

title: Predicting Stock Market time-series data using CNN-LSTM Neural Network model
short_description: This paper proposes a hybrid CNN-LSTM neural network model for stock market time-series prediction, extracting spatial features via CNN before passing them to LSTM for temporal modeling. The combined architecture achieves high accuracy even when trained on real-time data, outperforming standalone LSTM and other baseline models on standard performance metrics.

title: Profitability of Machine Learning Models in Forecasting the S&P 500 Index
authors: Dennis Olson, Salah Nusair, Emilios Galariotis
short_description: Evaluates fifteen ML models (incl. XGBoost, elastic nets, ensembles) on daily S&P 500 data 1970-2024 with rolling 10-year walk-forward training. Best models earn up to 24% annual abnormal returns pre-1987 but only ~9% after, and once realistic transaction costs are applied profits largely vanish post-1987 — consistent with the Adaptive Market Hypothesis. Notably honest on costs and lookahead, but reports best-of-fifteen models without a clear data-snooping correction.

Over 1997-2026 the standard least-squares signal delivered a Sharpe ratio of 0.6 and a Sortino ratio of 0.8 before costs (Sharpe stability ratio 2.7), with a representative book returning 6.1% annualised at 10.7% volatility and near-zero correlation to equities, bonds, and EURUSD forwards; transaction costs reduced the return only modestly to 5.8%. The study is fully reproducible via an attached Jupyter notebook built on J.P. Morgan's JPMaQS macro-quantamental data.

The volume-based representation classifies price patterns significantly better than a price-levels baseline (especially on liquid instruments), and tree-derived feature interactions closely match SHAP interaction values, validating SHAP interpretation in the financial-markets setting.

title: XGBoost for Classifying Ethereum Short-term Return Based on Technical Factor
Are Tick, Volume and Dollar Bars Truly Information-Driven? Validating Bar Sampling on SIP Data
short_description: Ahmed Nabil empirically tests whether tick, volume and dollar bars are truly information-driven using a three-month SIP trade dataset, showing 70.8% odd-lot prints and severe share-rank vs dollar-rank mismatches break volume and tick sampling on a modern internalized tape. He argues for dollar-runs bars, which close when the directional persistence of signed order flow becomes statistically surprising, isolating buy/sell asymmetry and detecting structural order-book changes before standard dollar bars breach their thresholds.

High-Frequency Market-Making with Inventory Constraints and Directional Bets

Introduces an inventory-risk-aversion parameter that allows the market-maker to place directional bets on price trends while controlling inventory risk, deriving closed-form optimal bid-ask quotes via Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equations. Monte Carlo simulations show the market-maker can increase PNL by over 15% versus the martingale benchmark or double her Sharpe ratio by tuning the risk-reward trade-off.

short_description: Ané and Geman show that normality of asset returns can be recovered through a stochastic time change, extending Clark (1973): the cumulative number of trades is a better stochastic clock than volume for generating virtually normal returns, and the clock can be modelled nonparametrically as a jump diffusion. Using tick-by-tick data on Cisco and Intel, the paper provides the statistical foundation for tick/information-driven bars — re-indexing returns by trade count collapses the fat-tailed calendar-time distribution toward Gaussian (a later comment by Murphy-Izzeldin notes higher moments are not perfectly recovered).

Sampling in volume time removes intra-session seasonality, partially recovers Normality and the IID assumption, and fixes the asynchronous-transaction problem in high-frequency correlation estimation; the paper traces the lineage to Mandelbrot-Taylor, Clark and Ané-Geman, introduces VPIN order-flow toxicity, and uses the 2010 flash crash as its cautionary case.

The paper reframes the classic low-state HMM failure to reproduce slow absolute-return autocorrelation decay as distributional (heavy tails) rather than temporal, and shows the fitted model yields a regime-conditional Value-at-Risk passing the Christoffersen conditional-coverage test plus a Student-t copula for multi-asset composition.

short_description: Using CRSP daily momentum decile portfolios over 1980-2025, the authors show that U.S. equity momentum returns concentrate in a six-trading-day window ending four days before month-end, where $1 invested in the value-weighted Winners-Minus-Losers portfolio grows to $18.78 versus only $2.37 during the rest of the month. The concentration is entirely loser-driven and is traced to predictable month-end cash demand: investors liquidate the most 'dispensable' loser stocks to raise liquidity, with bottom-decile losers underperforming the market by 7.9 basis points per day and about 70% of that underperformance reversing within a week. A 19-market replication and settlement-cycle reforms (T+2-to-T+1) confirm that momentum largely reflects predictable liquidity demand rather than under-reaction or priced risk.

title: Time Series Momentum
authors: Tobias J. Moskowitz, Yao Hua Ooi, and Lasse Heje Pedersen
short_description: This paper documents strong time-series and cross-sectional momentum in the long-short returns of a comprehensive sample of anomalies, finding that strategies exploiting this persistence deliver significant abnormal returns unexplained by traditional asset pricing models. The momentum in anomaly returns dissipates but does not reverse in the long run, consistent with limits-to-arbitrage and slow-moving capital, with the level and persistence of anomaly returns positively related to idiosyncratic volatility.

short_description: This paper documents consistent value and momentum return premia across eight diverse asset classes and markets, finding a strong common factor structure where value and momentum returns are more strongly correlated across asset classes than within them. The two factors are negatively correlated with each other across all asset classes, and global funding liquidity risk is identified as a partial source of these patterns, presenting a challenge to existing behavioral, institutional, and rational asset pricing theories.

title: Asset Allocation: Combining Investor Views with Market Equilibrium (Black-Litterman)
short_description: The canonical Bayesian portfolio model: equilibrium-implied returns (reverse-engineered from market-cap weights) form the prior, investor views with stated confidences are the evidence, and the conjugate posterior blends them — avoiding the extreme weights of naive mean-variance optimization. The most widely deployed instance of Bayesian updating in finance.

title: Bayes-Stein Estimation for Portfolio Analysis
short_description: Applies James-Stein/empirical-Bayes shrinkage to estimated expected returns, pulling each asset's mean toward the global minimum-variance portfolio mean with data-driven intensity. The practical workhorse of Bayesian portfolio estimation; dominates raw sample-estimate portfolios out-of-sample with more stable weights.

title: Bayesian Methods in Finance
short_description: Anchor handbook survey of Bayesian portfolio construction, mapping the canonical lineage: Klein-Bawa predictive-density optimization under parameter uncertainty, Jorion Bayes-Stein shrinkage, and Black-Litterman equilibrium priors (noting its missing likelihood, later fixed by Zhou). The best single map of how Bayesian updating threads through asset allocation.

title: Beyond Black-Litterman: Letting the Data Speak
short_description: Closes the main gap in Black-Litterman by adding a formal likelihood so the posterior reflects three sources — market equilibrium prior, subjective views, and historical-data evidence — making BL a fully coherent Bayesian updating scheme. The natural next read after the original Black-Litterman model.

short_description: This paper introduces YAND, a geometric optimization framework where search directions are derived from the equi-affine normal of level-set hypersurfaces, making them invariant under volume-preserving affine transformations. YAND achieves one-step convergence on convex quadratics (matching Newton's method), proves robust under arbitrarily ill-conditioned affine scalings, and establishes global, linear, and quadratic local convergence guarantees for general smooth objectives.

short_description: This paper integrates VMD preprocessing with simple linear models for time-series forecasting, evaluating the combined approach across 13 diverse datasets including ETTm2, M4, and Southeast Asian air quality data. The DLinear+VMD model achieves the lowest RMSE across all datasets in multivariate forecasting (average 0.019), demonstrating that VMD with simple linear models consistently outperforms complex neural network architectures.

title: WOA-VMD-FE-Transformer for Crude Oil Prices
authors: L. Yu, X. Zhang, Y. Lin et al.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Trading Factor Residuals
short_description: This paper replicates the Guijarro-Ordonez et al. (2019) Deep Learning Statistical Arbitrage methodology on U.S. equity data from 2016-2024, applying CNN and Transformer architectures to factor model residuals to identify mispricing opportunities. Out-of-sample Sharpe ratios occasionally exceeding 10 are observed, though the authors caution these may reflect model overfitting or highly specific market conditions requiring further robustness checks.

title: A Hidden Markov Model for Statistical Arbitrage in International Crude Oil Futures Markets
authors: Viviana Fanelli, Claudio Fontana, and Francesco Rotondi
short_description: This paper models the cointegration spread between Brent, WTI, and Shanghai crude oil futures as a mean-reverting regime-switching process driven by a hidden Markov chain, using online filter-based parameter estimators for strategy implementation. The analysis finds that arbitrage strategies involving Shanghai futures are profitable even under conservative transaction costs, and that incorporating the three-contract system enables arbitrage even when pairwise cointegration is not detected.

title: A Kalman Filter Approach to Pairs Trading with Non-Gaussian Heteroskedastic and Nonlinear Mean-Reverting Spread
short_description: Extends Kalman pairs trading beyond linear-Gaussian assumptions: the latent spread has non-Gaussian heteroskedastic innovations and nonlinear mean reversion, handled by a Quasi Monte Carlo Kalman filter (Gaussian-mixture approximation). The filtered spread is the trading indicator. Relevant to fat-tailed crypto spreads; unrefereed preprint.

title: A Markowitz Approach to Managing a Dynamic Basket of Moving-Band Statistical Arbitrages
authors: Kasper Johansson, Thomas Schmelzer, and Stephen Boyd
short_description: This paper applies the Markowitz optimization framework to manage a dynamic portfolio of moving-band statistical arbitrages (MBSAs), showing how to optimally size and combine multiple MBSA positions over time. Illustrated on recent historical data, the method achieves strong risk-adjusted returns that are essentially uncorrelated with the market.

title: A Pairs Trading Strategy Based on Linear State Space Models and the Kalman Filter
authors: Bernardo de Moura Pizzinga, Adrian Pizzinga, Jorge P. Zubelli
short_description: Pairs trading built on linear state-space models where the Kalman filter computes the conditional probability that the spread reverts to its long-term mean; trades trigger only on large reversion probabilities. A directly practitioner-oriented Bayesian signal design; authors caveat the empirical evidence as limited.

title: A Survey of Statistical Arbitrage Pairs Trading: Non-ML Methods, 2016-2023
short_description: This Warsaw University working paper surveys non-machine-learning statistical arbitrage pairs trading strategies published from 2016 to 2023, systematically categorizing cointegration-based, distance-based, stochastic spread, and other classical approaches. The survey provides a structured overview of methodology choices, empirical findings, and performance benchmarks across the recent literature on non-ML pairs trading.

title: Advanced Statistical Arbitrage with Reinforcement Learning
short_description: This paper introduces a model-free RL framework for statistical arbitrage that constructs mean-reverting spreads by minimizing an empirical mean reversion time metric, then uses RL to identify the optimal mean reversion trading strategy. The approach diverges from traditional methods by constructing state spaces that capture recent price movement trends and using reward functions tailored to mean reversion trading, applied to ten representative US stock pairs.

title: Altcoin-Bitcoin Arbitrage
Attention Factors for Statistical Arbitrage
authors: Elliot L. Epstein, Rose Wang, Jaewon Choi, and Markus Pelger

title: BTC-ETH Pairs Trading: Correlation Analysis and Statistical Arbitrage
Cluster-Based Pairs Trading: Combining Unsupervised Learning with Cointegration Filtering
Constructing Cointegrated Cryptocurrency Portfolios for Statistical Arbitrage
Copula-Based Trading of Cointegrated Cryptocurrency Pairs
short_description: This paper introduces a copula-based pairs trading strategy for cointegrated cryptocurrencies, applying linear and nonlinear cointegration tests with correlation measures to select pairs and fitting various copula families to generate mispricing signals. Backtesting across multiple position-opening triggers shows the proposed strategy outperforms buy-and-hold in both profitability and risk-adjusted returns.

title: Correlation Matrix Clustering for Statistical Arbitrage Portfolios
authors: Alvaro Cartea, Mihai Cucuringu, and Qi Jin

title: Crypto Pair Trading, Relative Value, and Basket Short Legs
short_description: Flip discusses crypto pair trading and relative-value basket construction using HYPE/SOL and LIT/ETH as examples. The post recommends rebalancing favorable spreads only when they drift materially from entry, avoiding isolated altcoin short legs, and using diversified short baskets because single-token announcements can create sharp idiosyncratic moves.

title: Deep Learning Statistical Arbitrage
authors: Jorge Guijarro-Ordonez, Markus Pelger, and Greg Zanotti
short_description: This paper applies a conditional factor model with a state space framework to a multivariate US equity portfolio for statistical arbitrage, using online filter-based estimates of time-varying risk premia to identify mean reversion trading candidates. Demonstrated over a 29-year empirical period with realistic transaction costs, the strategy remains competitive against cutting-edge approaches and continues to offer compelling economics in recent years.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Applied to Statistical Arbitrage Investment Strategy on Cryptomarket
Diversified Statistical Arbitrage: Dynamically Combining Mean Reversion and Momentum Strategies
End-to-End Policy Learning of a Statistical Arbitrage Autoencoder Architecture
short_description: This paper replaces PCA with an Autoencoder in statistical arbitrage to derive tradeable residuals with mean-reverting characteristics, then embeds the Autoencoder into a neural network policy trained end-to-end to directly optimize risk-adjusted portfolio returns. The end-to-end approach yields superior gross returns over classical two-stage methods and PCA/Fama-French baselines on US stock data.

title: Evaluation of Dynamic Cointegration-Based Pairs Trading Strategy in the Cryptocurrency Market
short_description: This paper demonstrates a dynamic cointegration-based pairs trading strategy with an optimal look-back window framework for cryptocurrency markets, using Engle-Granger, KSS, and Johansen cointegration tests calibrated via Ornstein-Uhlenbeck mean-reversion speed. The Johansen-assisted market-neutral strategy achieves 6.81% weekly returns versus 5.97% for EG2SLS on the Bitmex exchange, with reasonably low maximum drawdown.

title: Finding Moving-Band Statistical Arbitrages via Convex-Concave Optimization
authors: Kasper Johansson, Thomas Schmelzer, and Stephen Boyd
short_description: This paper proposes a convex-concave optimization method for finding statistical arbitrage portfolios with more than two assets, formulating the problem as maximizing portfolio volatility subject to its price remaining within a band and a leverage constraint. The sequential convex programming approach generalizes to moving-band statistical arbitrages where the band midpoint varies over time.

title: Graph Learning for Foreign Exchange Rate Prediction and Statistical Arbitrage
short_description: This paper proposes a two-step graph learning approach for FX rate prediction and statistical arbitrage, modeling multi-currency relationships as a spatiotemporal graph with currencies as nodes and FX rates as edges. The FXRP graph learning method yields statistically significant MSE improvements, and the FXSA method achieves a 61.89% higher information ratio and 45.51% higher Sortino ratio than the benchmark.

title: Harnessing Deep Q-Learning for Enhanced Statistical Arbitrage in High-Frequency Trading
short_description: This paper explores integrating deep Q-learning reinforcement learning into statistical arbitrage strategies for high-frequency trading scenarios, investigating exploration-exploitation trade-offs in non-stationary market environments. Extensive simulations and backtests reveal that RL enhances trading strategy adaptability and shows promise in improving profitability metrics and risk-adjusted returns compared to traditional HFT approaches.

title: High-Frequency Trading of Cryptocurrencies Through Short-Term Cointegration Pairs-Trading Strategies
short_description: This Erasmus University master's thesis applies cointegration-based pairs trading to cryptocurrency exchange rates using the Engle-Granger two-step (EG2SLS) and Johansen methods to form high-frequency trading portfolios. The Johansen-assisted strategy achieves 6.81% weekly returns versus 5.97% for EG2SLS over 16 weeks of cryptocurrency data, with lagging exchange rates identified as more likely to be cointegrated via Granger causality.

title: LLMs for Time Series: an Application for Single Stocks and Statistical Arbitrage
short_description: This paper challenges the common belief that LLMs are unsuitable for financial return prediction by applying the Chronos model to large-cap US stocks and constructing long/short statistical arbitrage portfolios. Performance simulations demonstrate that fine-tuned LLMs can identify market inefficiencies in near-random time series and generate alpha, with significant room for improvement remaining relative to benchmark models.

title: Neural Augmented Kalman Filtering with Bollinger Bands for Pairs Trading (KBPT)
authors: Amit Milstein, Haoran Deng, Guy Revach, Hai Morgenstern, Nir Shlezinger
short_description: Hybrid model-based deep learning for pairs trading: frames Kalman-filter + Bollinger Bands (KF-BB) as the model-based baseline, then proposes KBPT, an extended partial-cointegration state-space model augmented with a KalmanNet neural network. Reported to beat both classic KF-BB and a DDQN deep-RL benchmark across FX/ETF pairs (IEEE TSP 2024).

title: On Statistical Arbitrage Under a Conditional Factor Model of Equity Returns
authors: Trent Spears, Stefan Zohren, and Stephen Roberts
short_description: This paper tests for departures from NIID returns when the alternative hypothesis is self-affinity in financial asset returns, exploring both fractionally integrated long-range dependence and L-stable distribution alternatives. Using a simulation study to compare fluctuation analysis variants and applying the techniques to daily logarithmic returns across 11 stock market indices, the paper shows that a finite-sample fluctuation analysis method can identify both forms of self-affinity.

title: Optimal Entry and Exit with Signature in Statistical Arbitrage
authors: Boming Ning, Prakash Chakraborty, and Kiseop Lee
short_description: This paper formulates optimal entry and exit timing for mean-reverting spread trading as a sequential optimal stopping problem and applies a signature optimal stopping method to identify precise trade timing without predefined assumptions about spread dynamics. Numerical results demonstrate superior performance over conventional mean reversion rules while accounting for transaction costs.

title: Optimal Market-Neutral Multivariate Pair Trading on the Cryptocurrency Platform
Pairs Trading
authors: Robert J. Elliott, John van der Hoek, William P. Malcolm
short_description: Seminal state-space formulation of pairs trading: the spread is modeled as a mean-reverting Gaussian Markov chain (discrete OU process) observed in noise, estimated recursively by Kalman filter with EM calibration, and traded against the filter's one-step-ahead prediction. The analytical foundation for all Kalman-filter pairs strategies.

title: Pairs Trading - Selection via Scoring Systems
Pairs Trading and Statistical Arbitrage Across Top Cryptocurrencies
Pairs Trading with VectorBT® PRO

authors: Evan Gatev, William N. Goetzmann, and K. Geert Rouwenhorst

title: Quickly Make a Pairs Trading Strategy with 500 Stocks
Reinforcement Learning Pair Trading: A Dynamic Scaling Approach
short_description: This paper combines reinforcement learning with statistical arbitrage pair trading for cryptocurrencies, developing new reward shaping and observation/action spaces for RL agents trading BTC-GBP and BTC-EUR pairs at 1-minute intervals. RL-based traders achieve annualized profits from 9.94% to 31.53% depending on the learner, compared to 8.33% for traditional non-RL pair trading, demonstrating significant RL outperformance in volatile crypto markets.

title: Review of Statistical Arbitrage, Cointegration, and Multivariate Ornstein-Uhlenbeck
Short Scamtrash / Long Distinguished Crypto
Statistical Arbitrage Pairs Trading Strategies: Review and Outlook
Statistical Arbitrage Risk Premium by Machine Learning
short_description: This paper derives a theoretical result showing any risky asset can use a replicate portfolio of peer assets to hedge factor risks, defining the expected return of an asset's factor residual risk as the Statistical Arbitrage Risk Premium (SARP). Using elastic-net regression to construct replicate portfolios, high Statistical Arbitrage Risk stocks earn a monthly SARP of 1.101% greater than low SAR stocks, with the average SAR being countercyclical.

title: Statistical Arbitrage in Cryptocurrency Markets
authors: Nick Fischer, Christopher Krauss, and Alexander Deinert

title: Statistical Arbitrage in Multi-Pair Trading Strategy Based on Graph Clustering Algorithms in US Equities Market
short_description: This paper develops a statistical arbitrage strategy using graph clustering algorithms to identify multi-pair trading opportunities in US equities, combining Kelly criterion sizing with an ensemble of ML classifiers to improve signal quality and risk management. All tested approaches outperform benchmarks under realistic transaction costs, though performance is sensitive to changes in some key parameters.

title: Statistical Arbitrage in Options Markets by Graph Learning and Synthetic Long Positions
short_description: This paper proposes a two-stage graph learning approach for statistical arbitrage in options markets, using a tree-based RNConv architecture to predict mispricing targets and SLSA synthetic long positions to convert predictions into pure arbitrage opportunities neutral to all Black-Scholes risk factors. Experiments on KOSPI 200 index options show RNConv significantly outperforms GL baselines, with SLSA consistently yielding positive returns at an average P&L-contract information ratio of 0.1627.

title: Statistical Arbitrage in Polish Equities Market Using Deep Learning Techniques
short_description: This paper adapts the Avellaneda-Lee statistical arbitrage framework to the Polish WIG20 and mWIG40 stock market, replacing PCA with ETF replication and LSTM networks to construct factor residuals for mean-reversion trading. Backtesting over 2017-2019 shows the PCA approach achieves around 20% combined return with Sharpe ratio up to 2.63, while during the COVID-19 recession only ETF-based replication remains profitable at ~5% annual return.

title: Statistical Arbitrage in Rank Space
short_description: This paper proposes indexing stocks by market capitalization rank rather than company name to exploit enhanced mean-reverting properties of residual returns in rank space, implementing an intraday rebalancing mechanism for efficient name-to-rank portfolio conversion. Statistical arbitrage with neural networks in rank space significantly outperforms name space approaches, driven by a more robust market representation.

title: Statistical Arbitrage in the U.S. Equities Market
short_description: Decomposes U.S. stock returns into systematic and idiosyncratic components using PCA eigenportfolios or sector ETFs, models the residuals as Ornstein-Uhlenbeck processes, and trades contrarian s-score signals market-neutrally across stocks above USD 1 billion capitalisation. Net of 10 bps round-trip costs, PCA strategies average Sharpe 1.44 over 1997-2007 (decaying to 0.9 after 2003), volume-weighted trading-time ETF signals reach Sharpe 1.51 over 2003-2007, and a dedicated August 2007 chapter validates the Khandani-Lo unwinding theory of the quant meltdown.

title: Statistical Arbitrage within Crypto Markets using PCA
Trading and Arbitrage in Cryptocurrency Markets
100 Trading Indicators Tested Across 4,100+ Strategies
short_description: Tomas Nesnidal tests 100 indicator conditions across 4,115 NASDAQ and Bitcoin breakout strategies with in-sample and out-of-sample validation. The study finds that simple volatility and participation measures, especially bar range relative to ATR, dominate breakout filters, while oscillators such as RSI and stochastics consistently degrade performance.

A Short-Term Breakout Trading Strategy for Cryptocurrencies
Are Simple Technical Trading Rules Profitable in Bitcoin Markets?

Evaluating RSI as a Momentum Signal for Bitcoin and Altcoins

Using a simulation study to compare fluctuation analysis variants and applying the techniques to daily logarithmic returns across 11 stock market indices, the paper shows that a finite-sample fluctuation analysis method can identify both forms of self-affinity.

title: ARMA and ARIMA Models
Bayesian Forecasting in Economics and Finance: A Modern Review
authors: Gael M. Martin, David T. Frazier, Worapree Maneesoonthorn, Ruben Loaiza-Maya, Florian Huber, Gary Koop, John Maheu, Didier Nibbering, Anastasios Panagiotelis
short_description: Peer-reviewed, implementation-aware modern survey of Bayesian forecasting (IJF 2024). Covers MCMC, variational/approximate Bayes, and — most relevant to state-space trading models — a full algorithmic treatment of sequential Monte Carlo / particle filtering including particle marginal Metropolis-Hastings (PMMH). The recommended modern anchor for Bayesian time-series methods in trading.

title: Cointegration and Error Correction Models
Forecasting with Hyper-Trees
short_description: März and Rasul repurpose gradient boosted decision trees from forecasting time series directly to learning the parameters of classical time series models (AR, ARMA, ETS, STL, VAR) as functions of features, transferring the hypernetwork concept to GBDTs. The approach gains extrapolation, cross-series learning, time-varying interpretable parameters and full GBDT tabular functionality; a hybrid Hyper-TreeNet (GBDT encoder + shallow MLP decoder) keeps runtime flat as parameter counts grow. On public benchmarks Hyper-Tree-AR wins Air Passengers across all metrics and is the most accurate model on Rossmann Store Sales, beating conventional GBDTs on their home turf.

title: Fractional Differencing: How to Save Time Series Memory
Matrix Profiles for Pattern Recognition in Financial Time Series
short_description: Andreas Steiner demonstrates a Matrix Profile-based pattern-recognition engine for financial time series, built as a transparent Excel toolkit (ApaLibNET) rather than a black-box ML model. Using daily prices of a Swiss insurer, it computes per-window nearest-neighbour distances to surface recurring motifs and discords (anomalies), compares standardised 10-bar shapes, and overlays profile values on cumulative returns to reveal regime signatures and recurring micro-structures.

title: On Covariance Estimation of Non-Synchronously Observed Diffusion Processes
Time Series Fundamentals
Time-Series Forecasting: Deep Learning vs Statistics - Who Comes Out on Top?
Topological Data Analysis of Financial Time Series: Landscapes of Crashes
short_description: Applies persistent homology to US stock index returns, finding persistence landscape norms exhibit strong growth ~250 trading days prior to the 2000 and 2008 crashes.

Transformer Models for Price Prediction: Beyond LSTM

A Century of Evidence on Trend-Following Investing
A Filtering Method that Increased Crypto Trend-Following Win Rate to 60%
A Trend Following Framework for Gold, Bitcoin, and Nasdaq
Applying Traditional Managed Futures Trend-Following to Crypto Markets
Basic Trend Following
Catching Crypto Trends: A Tactical Approach for Bitcoin and Altcoins
Combining Trend Signals with Volatility Targeting for 1,127% Return at 67% Less Risk
Demystifying Managed Futures
authors: Brian Hurst, Yao Hua Ooi, and Lasse Heje Pedersen
short_description: This paper shows that returns of Managed Futures funds and CTAs can be explained by simple time series momentum strategies, demonstrating empirically that trend-following strategies produce large correlations and high R-squares with Managed Futures indices. Controlling for time series momentum strategies drives the alphas of even the largest Managed Futures managers to zero, with the paper also discussing implementation aspects including risk management, asset allocation, and transaction costs.

title: Diagnosing and Fixing an Underperforming Trend Model
Do Breakouts of Strong Swings Work? Testing 40 Futures Markets
Does Adding a Short Book Improve Crypto Trend Following Returns?

How Relaxing Signal Selection Constraints Doubled Strategy Returns
How to Manage an Intraday Trend Trade
Is Trend Still Your Friend? A Microstructural Account of the Demise of Short-Term Trend-Following
authors: Jutta G. Kurth, Zoltan Eisler, Adam Rej, Jean-Philippe Bouchaud
short_description: Kurth, Eisler, Rej and Bouchaud (CFM) explain the post-2009 demise of short-term trend-following using a cross-section of ~100 liquid futures over 1995-2025. Their central finding is that the volatility-normalised tick size separates degraded from surviving trends: post-2008 trend PnL collapsed on small-tick contracts (fast EWM-5-20 Sharpe fell from ~0.84 to 0.12) while holding up on large-tick ones, which they attribute to HFT-dominated market making breaking the self-fulfilling price-impact loop that sustains trend.

title: Leverage for the Long Run - A Systematic Approach to Managing Risk and Magnifying Returns in Stocks
authors: Michael A. Gayed and Charlie Bilello

Two Centuries of Trend Following
Using Lower-Timeframe Exits to Improve Trend-Following Risk Management
Using Volume and Market Cap Rankings to Improve Crypto Trend Signal Selection
Volatility Targeting: Transforming a -70% Drawdown Strategy into 1,328% Returns
You Can't Always Trend When You Want
authors: Abhilash Babu, Brendan Hoffman, Ari Levine, Yao Hua Ooi, Sarah Schroeder, and Erik Stamelos
short_description: This AQR paper presents a framework to decompose trend-following performance into (1) the magnitude of market moves, (2) the strategy's ability to profit from those moves, and (3) diversification across markets. The authors find that recent below-average trend performance is primarily explained by smaller average global market moves rather than reduced ability to profit from trends or lack of diversification, suggesting trend following may recover in environments with more pronounced market movements.

title: A Simple Approximate Long-Memory Model of Realized Volatility
A devilish question for option sellers: Which VRP is higher?
short_description: An X article by Kris Abdelmessih (Moontower) arguing that the volatility risk premium an option seller actually earns depends critically on measurement — ratio (IV/RV) versus spread (IV minus RV) — and on the distinction between volatility and variance, since a short option's P&L scales with the gap in squared vols. Using HYG as the running example, it shows that ratio screens flatter low-vol names: theta-neutral sizing equalizes decay but not variance edge (which requires the square of the theta ratio), so a 10-vol name would have to trade near 17.9 vol to match a 50-vol name at a 20% premium. The takeaway is that variance is the 'V' that matters, vol-point spreads rank opportunities better than ratios, and harvesting low-vol premium is costlier in contracts traded and gamma hedging.

title: Comparing Deep Learning Models for the Task of Volatility Prediction Using Multivariate Data
authors: Wenbo Ge, Pooia Lalbakhsh, Leigh Isai, Artem Lensky, and Hanna Suominen

title: DeepVol: Volatility Forecasting from High-Frequency Data with Dilated Causal Convolutions
Detecting Structural Evolution of Implied Volatility Surface Using Gradient-Based Features
short_description: An Aalto University master's thesis that develops a model-free method for detecting structural changes in the S&P 500 implied volatility surface by computing local gradients (partial derivatives of implied volatility with respect to moneyness and maturity) and clustering their daily changes with HDBSCAN. Using SPX option data from 2007 to 2025, it finds that the majority of daily surface changes are noise while a small number of recurring clusters (up to 18 observations each) capture distinct, interpretable skew and term-structure regimes, providing a framework for monitoring regime shifts across the entire volatility surface.

title: Early Birds Get the Vol: Morning Volatility Uncertainty and Variance Risk Premium
Faster Volatility Estimators and the Gross-versus-Net Trap
short_description: A progress-note X thread from DeltaDog on building a risk model, finding that fast, short-lookback EWMA realized-volatility estimators consistently beat slow ones on daily data. The follow-up discussion warns this "faster always wins" result may be an artifact of unrealistically low modelled trading costs — the net (after-cost) equity curve tracks the gross (before-cost) one too closely to penalise the fast estimators' higher turnover, so a practitioner may deliberately blend in slower estimators for robustness.

title: Forecasting Realised Volatility Using Regime-Switching Models
authors: Yi Ding, Dimos S. Kambouroudis, and David G. McMillan

title: Forecasting Realized Volatility in Turbulent Times using Temporal Fusion Transformers
Gammega at +3.5σ: When Spot and IV Pull Apart
short_description: Cayo Largo Research analyzes a BTC options episode where spot rallied while 30-day ATM IV fell, causing Gammega, a fourth-order Greek tied to Gamma vol-sensitivity, to rise before first-order Gamma exposure reacted. The note argues that this cross-derivative stress can reveal gamma instability hours before standard Greek dashboards flag the risk.

title: HARNet: A Convolutional Neural Network for Realized Volatility Forecasting
authors: Rafael Reisenhofer, Xandro Bayer, and Nikolaus Hautsch

title: Harvesting the Variance Risk Premium: A Backtest Across Expiry-Day, Stop-Loss and Lot-Scaling Dimensions
short_description: A cost-aware backtest of short-volatility strategies designed to harvest the variance risk premium (option-implied volatility exceeding subsequently realized volatility) in short-dated index options, run on one-minute options-chain and index data from September 2024 to June 2026 across expiry-day, stop-loss, and lot-scaling dimensions. The main finding is that positive gross expectancy is erased by transaction costs and taxes at a single lot and only becomes net-positive once positions are scaled up to amortize fixed per-trade costs; the strategies also respond poorly to stop-losses. The author concludes the edge is real but the barriers to capturing it are equally real for retail traders.

title: Introducing NBEATSx to Realized Volatility Forecasting
authors: Hugo Gobato Souto and Amir Moradi

title: Meta-Learning Neural Process for Implied Volatility Surfaces with SABR-induced Priors
Normalizing volatility transform for Normal implied volatility
short_description: Introduces a normalizing volatility transform (NVT), f(k)=k/sigma(k), for the Normal (Bachelier) implied volatility model and uses it to characterise static arbitrage. Proves conditions for the absence of vertical-spread, butterfly, large-strike and calendar arbitrage, shows the Normal case needs an extra put-price condition versus the log-normal setting, and that an arbitrage-free surface requires the total normal implied volatility to be non-decreasing in maturity.

title: Volatility Modeling: A Deep Dive into the ARCH Family
Volatility-Managed Portfolios
short_description: This paper shows that managed portfolios which scale down risk exposure when recent volatility is high produce large alphas and substantially increase Sharpe ratios across market, value, momentum, profitability, return on equity, and investment factors as well as the currency carry trade. The finding that volatility timing improves performance even while taking less risk in recessions and crises challenges both conventional wisdom and structural models of time-varying expected returns.

title: Why Your Volatility Forecast Matters More Than You Think
